channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump to allies who need access to Strait of Hormuz: 'Go get your own oil'
item.description: President Trump tells allies who rely on the Strait of Hormuz for oil, "Go get your own oil," as the U.S. backs off promises to open the strait.
item.content:encoded: President Trump tells allies who rely on the Strait of Hormuz for oil, "Go get your own oil," as the U.S. backs off promises to open the strait.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: Former special envoy on Trump's comments that Iran war could be over in weeks
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Robert Malley, former special envoy to Iran in the Biden administration, about President Trump's comments that the U.S. could end the Iran conflict in a matter of weeks.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Robert Malley, former special envoy to Iran in the Biden administration, about President Trump's comments that the U.S. could end the Iran conflict in a matter of weeks.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Trump's executive order on mail voting is set to face legal challenges
item.description: An executive order from President Trump seeks to create federal lists of eligible voters and instructs the Postal Service to send ballots only to approved voters. It faces certain legal challenges.
item.content:encoded: An executive order from President Trump seeks to create federal lists of eligible voters and instructs the Postal Service to send ballots only to approved voters. It faces certain legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump tells allies who need Strait of Hormuz for oil to get it themselves, how the Iran war is impacting the U.S. and global economy, SCOTUS to hear arguments on birthright citizenship.
item.content:encoded: Trump tells allies who need Strait of Hormuz for oil to get it themselves, how the Iran war is impacting the U.S. and global economy, SCOTUS to hear arguments on birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: After years in Russian captivity, former mayor of Ukrainian city returns home
item.description: After spending most of the full-scale war in Russian captivity, the former mayor of a frontline Ukrainian city recalls his ordeal as he returns to a hometown deeply transformed by modern warfare.
item.content:encoded: After spending most of the full-scale war in Russian captivity, the former mayor of a frontline Ukrainian city recalls his ordeal as he returns to a hometown deeply transformed by modern warfare.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: NASA readies for historic Artemis II moon launch
item.description: Artemis II, the first crewed mission to the Moon in more than 50 years, is set to launch as early as 6:24 p.m. Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: Artemis II, the first crewed mission to the Moon in more than 50 years, is set to launch as early as 6:24 p.m. Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Judge rules White House ballroom construction must stop until Congress approves it
item.description: A judge ruled Tuesday that construction on President Trump's White House ballroom "must stop until Congress authorizes its completion."
item.content:encoded: A judge ruled Tuesday that construction on President Trump's White House ballroom "must stop until Congress authorizes its completion."
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Groups work to give people living in rural areas with Parkinson's access to care
item.description: Many people diagnosed with Parkinson's disease live in rural areas where it can be tough to access services they need, but dedicated individuals are working to make it easier.
item.content:encoded: Many people diagnosed with Parkinson's disease live in rural areas where it can be tough to access services they need, but dedicated individuals are working to make it easier.
item.dc:creator: Bek Shackelford

item.title: Some Iranians express pessimism over the war and the potential for regime change
item.description: Iranians coming across the border into Turkey are less hopeful than they were at the beginning of the war.
item.content:encoded: Iranians coming across the border into Turkey are less hopeful than they were at the beginning of the war.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on birthright citizenship
item.description: Supreme Court justices are set to hear arguments on Wednesday in a challenge President Trump brought to the longstanding legal protections for citizenship conveyed to every child born in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: Supreme Court justices are set to hear arguments on Wednesday in a challenge President Trump brought to the longstanding legal protections for citizenship conveyed to every child born in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg
